name: Bouillabaisse Et Rouille
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Assorted white fish (sea bass, flounder, red snapper, grouper, perch, sole, pike, haddock, cod)
3 lb Clean fish bones (remove fins, skin, etc., and all traces of blood)
5 large Tomatoes (peeled, cored, seeded, and chopped)
1 Bouquet garni (1 bay leaf, 1 thyme sprig, 6 parsley sprigs, pinch each of grated orange peel, ground fennel seeds, basil, oregano)
2 Baguettes (French bread) cut diagonally into thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Fillet the fish or have your fishmonger do it for you. Save the bones.
2. Cut the fish into 1-inch (2.5-cm) chunks and marinate with 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 clove chopped garlic, a few saffron threads, and a pinch of thyme for at least 1 hour.
3. In a large skillet or pot, heat 4 tablespoons olive oil over medium heat. Add the onions and remaining chopped garlic. Cook for 4 minutes until fragrant.
4. Add the fish bones, tomatoes, bouquet garni, orange peel, fennel seeds, basil, oregano, carrots, leeks, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
5. Add the water and 1 cup of white wine. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes, crushing bones occasionally with a wooden spoon.
6. Strain the broth through a fine mesh strainer into a soup pot, discarding solids.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. If thin, reduce over low heat until desired consistency is reached.
8. Add the clams and simmer until they just begin to open. Add the fish and remaining wine. Return to a rolling boil for 10 minutes.
9. Stir in saffron threads and continue cooking for another 5 minutes.
10. Season with Pernod, salt, and pepper. Divide soup among warmed bowls.
11. In separate dishes or on top of the soup, dry baguette slices briefly, then rub with whole garlic cloves. Top generously with rouille and place slices in the soups.
12. Serve immediately.
